name: FINOS CALM Toolchain
description: Architecture of the FINOS CALM toolchain monorepo, including the CALM Hub backend, Hub UI, validation server, CLI, and supporting ecosystem services. Discovered via static analysis of the codebase.

metadata.note: Initial discovery via static analysis — review and validate before use in production

nodes.name: Developer / Architect
nodes.description: Human user who creates, validates, and explores CALM architecture models via the Hub UI, CLI, desktop studio, or IDE extension

nodes.name: AI Assistant
nodes.description: AI agents (Claude, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Kiro) that interact with the CALM Hub via the MCP endpoint to assist with architecture authoring and discovery

nodes.name: CALM Hub UI
nodes.description: React 19 single-page application providing a browser-based interface to browse, create, and visualise CALM architectures, patterns, flows, controls, and decorators. Vite dev server proxies API calls to the CALM Hub backend on port 8080

nodes.name: CALM Documentation Site
nodes.description: Docusaurus v3 static documentation website for the FINOS CALM project. Built by CI/CD and published to AWS S3 for public access

nodes.name: CALM Studio
nodes.description: Electron-based cross-platform desktop application for creating and exploring CALM architecture models locally without a running Hub instance

nodes.name: CALM Hub
nodes.description: Java/Quarkus REST API backend providing full CRUD for CALM namespaces, architectures, patterns, flows, controls, decorators, standards, and ADRs. Also exposes an MCP (Model Context Protocol) endpoint at /mcp for AI assistant integrations. Supports pluggable persistence via MongoDB (production) or embedded NitriteDB (standalone)

nodes.name: CALM Validation Server
nodes.description: Node.js/Express standalone validation server exposing POST /calm/validate. Validates CALM architecture JSON against bundled schemas and Spectral rules. Runs on port 3000 by default, bound to 127.0.0.1

nodes.name: CALM CLI
nodes.description: Node.js command-line tool (calm) for generating architectures from patterns, validating models, running Handlebars templates, generating documentation sites, and initialising AI assistant configurations via calm init-ai

nodes.name: CALM VSCode Extension
nodes.description: Visual Studio Code extension providing CALM-aware JSON editing, inline validation feedback, and AI-assisted architecture authoring within the IDE

nodes.name: CALM Guard
nodes.description: Standalone service responsible for CALM policy enforcement or governance checks. Exact responsibilities inferred from a dedicated CI workflow (build-calm-guard.yml); requires further investigation

nodes.name: CALM JSON Schemas
nodes.description: Versioned JSON Schema files (calm.json, core.json, interface.json, flow.json, control.json, timeline.json, etc.) stored under /calm/release/ and /calm/draft/. Consumed by all validators, the CLI, and the Hub for schema resolution and validation

nodes.name: MongoDB
nodes.description: MongoDB database (port 27017, database calmSchemas) used as the primary persistent store for all CALM Hub resources in production and containerised deployments

nodes.name: NitriteDB
nodes.description: Embedded NoSQL database persisted to ~/.calm-hub/data, used as the CALM Hub store in standalone or development mode. No external service required; selected at runtime via calm.database.mode configuration

nodes.name: Keycloak (OIDC Provider)
nodes.description: External identity provider offering OAuth2/OIDC authentication for the CALM Hub backend and UI when running in the secure deployment profile. Dev instance runs on port 9443

nodes.name: Docker Hub
nodes.description: Public container image registry. CALM Hub multi-arch images (finos/calm-hub:latest) are built for amd64 and arm64 and pushed here by the docker-publish-calm-hub.yml CI workflow on release

nodes.name: AWS S3
nodes.description: Cloud object storage used to host the published CALM documentation site and video assets. Synced by GitHub Actions workflows s3-docs-sync.yml and s3-video-sync.yml

nodes.name: GitHub / GitHub Actions
nodes.description: Source control and CI/CD platform. GitHub Actions orchestrates all build, test, security scanning (CVE, licence, Semgrep), Docker publishing, and release workflows for every package in the monorepo

nodes.name: Kubernetes Cluster
nodes.description: Container orchestration cluster hosting the calmhub Deployment (exposed via LoadBalancer on port 80→8080) and the mongodb Deployment (exposed internally via ClusterIP on port 27017). Raw manifests provided under calm-hub/k8s/

relationships.description: Reads CALM project documentation, guides, and schema references published to the documentation site
Runs policy enforcement or governance checks against CALM architecture documents
Browses, creates, and visualises CALM architectures through the web interface
Runs generate, validate, docify, and template commands from the terminal
Authors and explores CALM models using the local desktop application
Edits and validates CALM files within the IDE with real-time feedback
Invokes CALM Hub MCP tools (list/create architectures, decorators, namespaces, search) via Streamable HTTP at /mcp
Sends Axios HTTP requests to /calm/namespaces/* REST endpoints for architecture, pattern, flow, control, decorator, and search operations
Pulls remote schemas and publishes architectures to the CALM Hub REST API
Sends architecture JSON to POST /calm/validate for schema and Spectral validation
Reads and writes CALM documents via the Hub REST API from the desktop application
Calls the validation server to provide inline schema validation feedback inside the editor
Persists and retrieves all CALM resources using the MongoDB Java driver over the wire protocol
Reads and writes CALM resources via the embedded Nitrite driver to the local filesystem when running in standalone mode
Performs OAuth2 authorisation code flow and token refresh against the OIDC provider using oidc-client-ts
Validates JWT bearer tokens by introspecting against the Keycloak JWKS endpoint via the Quarkus OIDC extension in the secure profile
Loads bundled CALM JSON Schema files from dist/calm/ at startup to perform architecture validation
Reads schema files from the local schema directory or fetches them remotely for generate and validate operations
Builds multi-arch CALM Hub container images and pushes finos/calm-hub to Docker Hub on release via the docker-publish-calm-hub.yml workflow
Uploads the built Docusaurus documentation site and video assets to S3 via s3-docs-sync.yml and s3-video-sync.yml workflows
CALM Hub runs as a Kubernetes Deployment exposed via a LoadBalancer service on port 80 mapping to container port 8080
MongoDB runs as a Kubernetes Deployment exposed internally via a ClusterIP service on port 27017
